title: rootless: secret not accessible to non root
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Hello podman team,

My container cannot access secrets when running as non root.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```sh
#!/bin/sh
set -o xtrace

SECRET="mysecret"
echo a_secret | podman secret create "$SECRET" -
podman run --secret "$SECRET" --rm docker.io/library/debian sh -c "id && cat /run/secrets/$SECRET"
podman run --secret "$SECRET" --rm --user 200:200 docker.io/library/debian sh -c "id && cat /run/secrets/$SECRET"
podman secret rm "$SECRET"
```

**Describe the results you received:**

The user 200:200 received the error `cat: /run/secrets/mysecret: Permission denied` when accessing the secret.

```
+ SECRET=mysecret
+ echo a_secret
+ podman secret create mysecret -
200255d821a5ef5b0a3e8b7b6
+ podman run --secret mysecret --rm docker.io/library/debian sh -c 'id && cat /run/secrets/mysecret'
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
a_secret
+ podman run --secret mysecret --rm --user 200:200 docker.io/library/debian sh -c 'id && cat /run/secrets/mysecret'
uid=200(200) gid=200(200) groups=200(200)
cat: /run/secrets/mysecret: Permission denied
+ podman secret rm mysecret
200255d821a5ef5b0a3e8b7b6
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The user 200:200 should access the secret.

```
+ SECRET=mysecret
+ echo a_secret
+ podman secret create mysecret -
200255d821a5ef5b0a3e8b7b6
+ podman run --secret mysecret --rm docker.io/library/debian sh -c 'id && cat /run/secrets/mysecret'
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
a_secret
+ podman run --secret mysecret --rm --user 200:200 docker.io/library/debian sh -c 'id && cat /run/secrets/mysecret'
uid=200(200) gid=200(200) groups=200(200)
a_secret
+ podman secret rm mysecret
200255d821a5ef5b0a3e8b7b6
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Git Commit:   51b8ddbc22cf5b10dd76dd9243924aa66ad7db39
Built:        Wed Apr 21 14:34:03 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 10000
      size: 256
  kernel: 5.12.1-arch1-1
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 416268288
  memTotal: 16704000000
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: /usr/bin/crun is owned by crun 0.19.1-1
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.19.1
      commit: 1535fedf0b83fb898d449f9680000f729ba719f5
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true

    selinuxEnabled: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.1
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 178h 4m 35.83s (Approximately 7.42 days)

Yes

physical

comments.body: @ashley-cui Secrets should probably be world readable within the container.

@sbourlon: The label(s) `kind/bug` cannot be applied, because the repository doesn't have them.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@sbourlon Interested in opening a PR to fix this?

@rhatdan Not sure but I think issue is with **libpod**. @sbourlon I saw this earlier but I ignored as thought there is a issue with my local configuration but this patch should do the trick. Would you like to verify it yourself ? https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10347
I agree, I think the secret is being added to the container with tight protection.

I have a PR ready to be open on monday that should address this issue as well as https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10210, where it will fix the default UID, GID, and mode and let rootless access the secret correctly, as well as let the user set it via options passed in the secrets flag. I think we should wait for that PR instead of https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10347 since it adds functionality as well. It just needs a few more tests to check for this issue as well: https://github.com/ashley-cui/podman/commit/9a61c479c0f55f4ce806cc65ee7fb302bfb2cda5

I guess another question is should the secret be accessible to non-root inside the container by default, or should the user have to specify --secret uid=1000 for the user to be able to access the secret?

@ashley-cui @rhatdan No issues let me know if https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10347 needs to be closed.

@ashley-cui For second question I think we should have it since it makes things effortless and intuitive for admins who prefer `non-root` user as default and also it'll be really cool feature to have, but make only `non-root` user specified by **admin context** owner/access of `/run/secrets` while `launching`/`running` container not all `non-root` users.  I guess that is what @sbourlon was trying to do in the first place.

Thank you everyone for all the replies.

Maybe options following the style of the `--mount` parameter would be good (`--mount=type=TYPE,TYPE-SPECIFIC-OPTION[,...]`)?

`--secret=secret[,mode=400,owner=$USER]`

with:
- mode: the same octal mode as the chmod command, defaulting to 400 to keep it secured by default
- owner: the same owner[:group] as the chown command, defaulting to `--user`

Another interesting side effect of `mode` is the caller can set it to 700 to be able to delete the secret file once loaded in memory avoiding the secret to leak to an attacker getting access to the container.

Thank you so much @ashley-cui for the fix.

For testing, I built the master branch using the command `make podman`.

`bin/podman info`:
```
...
version:
  APIVersion: 3.2.0-dev
  Built: 1621335485
  BuiltTime: Tue May 18 03:58:05 2021
  GitCommit: d1d21f069a51fd51bc4ecdc1e003add4eea6c9a6
  GoVersion: go1.16.4
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.2.0-dev
```

I was hoping to be able to delete the secret with the following options: `--secret secret,uid=200,gid=200,mode=0600`

My test:
```
% bin/podman run --secret secret,uid=200,gid=200,mode=0600 --rm --user 200:200 -it docker.io/library/debian bash -c "set -x; ls -la /run/secrets; cat /run/secrets/secret; rm /run/secrets/secret; cat /run/secrets/secret"
+ ls -la /run/secrets
total 8
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root   60 May 18 11:18 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 May 18 11:18 ..
-rw------- 1 200  200     9 May 18 11:18 secret
+ cat /run/secrets/secret
mysecret
+ rm /run/secrets/secret
rm: cannot remove '/run/secrets/secret': Permission denied
+ cat /run/secrets/secret
mysecret
```
@sbourlon hm okay let me take another look
@sbourlon This behavior is consistent to docker's secret behavior.

Just for the record for people interested in removing the secret, it can be removed by writing into its file:
```
% bin/podman run --secret secret,uid=200,gid=200,mode=1600 --rm --user 200:200 -it docker.io/library/debian bash -c "set -x; ls -la /run/secrets; cat /run/secrets/secret; echo > /run/secrets/secret; cat /run/secrets/secret; echo done"
+ ls -la /run/secrets
total 8
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root   60 May 18 20:21 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 May 18 20:21 ..
-rw------- 1 200  200     9 May 18 20:21 secret
+ cat /run/secrets/secret
mysecret
+ echo
+ cat /run/secrets/secret

+ echo done
done
```

@ashley-cui thank you again for the quick fix

Hello @ashley-cui 

Do you know if this feature is in v3.2.2? It seems this version is from Jun 25 while your feature was merged on May 17.

Test:
```
#!/usr/bin/env bash
podman --version
SECRET="mysecret"
echo a_secret | podman secret create "$SECRET" -
echo "> as root"
podman run --rm --secret "$SECRET" -it docker.io/library/bash cat "/run/secrets/$SECRET"
echo "> as non root without the uid option"
podman run --rm --secret "$SECRET" --user 200:200 -it docker.io/library/bash cat "/run/secrets/$SECRET"
echo "> as non root with the uid option"podman run --rm --secret "$SECRET",uid=200 --user 200:200 -it docker.io/library/bash cat "/run/secrets/$SECRET"
podman secret rm "$SECRET"
```

Outputs:
```
podman version 3.2.2
e53b1a455d9ceb057946d523a
> as root
a_secret
> as non root without the uid option
cat: can't open '/run/secrets/mysecret': Permission denied
> as non root with the uid option
Error: option uid=200 invalid: error parsing secret
e53b1a455d9ceb057946d523a
```
@sbourlon oops, I don't think it made it into v3.2.2. I'll be sure it makes it in the next release
